# load the quranic_verses.json and inspect some entries to understand structure
import json, os, sys, unicodedata, re
p = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
with open(p, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
len(data), type(data[:3])

# examine first few entries
for i in range(5):
    print(i, data[i].keys())
    print(data[i].get('ayah_text')[:120])
    print()

# Let's implement normalization and search for occurrences of substring 'استضعف' in quranic verses.
import re, unicodedata
def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tatweel, diacritics, punctuation, unify hamza
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # normalize NFKC
    text = unicodedata.normalize("NFC", text)
    # remove tashkeel (diacritics)
    arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]')
    text = re.sub(arabic_diacritics, '', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640','')
    # unify hamza forms
    text = text.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا').replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي')
    # map dotless alef? leave
    # remove punctuation marks and non letters/digits (but keep arabic letters)
    # remove parentheses, commas, braces, quotes, punctuation like ؛،.?:!
    text = re.sub(r'[\u060C\u061B\u061F\u066A-\u066D\u06D4\!\?\:\.\,\-\(\)\[\]\{\}#٪\"\'/\\\—\–\~\…]',' ',text)
    # collapse spaces
    text = re.sub(r'\s+',' ',text).strip()
    # lowercase? Arabic has no case
    return text

candidates = []
for entry in data:
    nt = normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    if 'استضعف' in nt or 'استضعفو' in nt or 'الاستضعاف' in nt:
        candidates.append((entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['ayah_text'], nt[:200]))
len(candidates), candidates[:10]

# Let's compute similarity between the FULL substring and candidate 33:35
full_sub = "إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ الَّذِينَ اسْتَضْعَفُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَنْفُسَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَأَنْفُسَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ"
candidate = None
for entry in data:
    if entry['surah_id']==33 and entry['ayah_id']==35:
        candidate = entry['ayah_text']
        break
print("Candidate:", candidate)
def tokenize_ar(text):
    txt = normalize_ar(text)
    tokens = txt.split()
    return tokens
tokens_full = tokenize_ar(full_sub)
tokens_cand = tokenize_ar(candidate)
tokens_full, tokens_cand[:40], len(tokens_full), len(tokens_cand)

from collections import Counter, defaultdict
set_full = Counter(tokens_full)
set_cand = Counter(tokens_cand)
intersection = sum((set_full & set_cand).values())
precision = intersection / sum(set_full.values())
recall = intersection / sum(set_cand.values())
f1 = 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall) if (precision+recall)>0 else 0
intersection, precision, recall, f1

# compute char-level LCS (Longest Common Subsequence) between normalized strings
def lcs_length(a,b):
    # dynamic programming; be careful about sizes.
    n, m = len(a), len(b)
    # optimize memory using two rows
    prev = [0]*(m+1)
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        cur = [0]*(m+1)
        ai = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1,m+1):
            if ai == b[j-1]:
                cur[j] = prev[j-1]+1
            else:
                cur[j] = prev[j] if prev[j] >= cur[j-1] else cur[j-1]
        prev = cur
    return prev[m]

full_norm = normalize_ar(full_sub)
cand_norm = normalize_ar(candidate)
lcs_len = lcs_length(full_norm, cand_norm)
lcs_ratio_a = lcs_len / max(len(full_norm), 1)
lcs_ratio_b = lcs_len / max(len(cand_norm), 1)
lcs_len, lcs_ratio_a, lcs_ratio_b, full_norm, cand_norm[:200]

# Let's search all ayahs and compute token F1 and char-LCS ratio (w.r.t candidate or input)
full_norm = normalize_ar(full_sub)
tokens_full = full_norm.split()

def token_f1(a_tokens, b_tokens):
    a_counts = Counter(a_tokens)
    b_counts = Counter(b_tokens)
    inter = sum((a_counts & b_counts).values())
    if inter==0:
        return 0.0
    prec = inter / sum(a_counts.values())
    rec = inter / sum(b_counts.values())
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec) if (prec+rec)>0 else 0.0
    return f1, prec, rec

best = []
for entry in data:
    cand = entry['ayah_text']
    cand_norm = normalize_ar(cand)
    tokens_cand = cand_norm.split()
    f1, prec, rec = token_f1(tokens_full, tokens_cand)
    lcs_len = lcs_length(full_norm, cand_norm)
    lcs_ratio_input = lcs_len / len(full_norm) if len(full_norm)>0 else 0
    lcs_ratio_cand = lcs_len / len(cand_norm) if len(cand_norm)>0 else 0
    # create composite similarity as max of token f1, lcs_ratio_input, lcs_ratio_cand
    composite = max(f1, lcs_ratio_input, lcs_ratio_cand)
    best.append((composite, f1, lcs_ratio_input, lcs_ratio_cand, entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], cand))
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:10]

def token_f12(a_tokens, b_tokens):
    a_counts = Counter(a_tokens)
    b_counts = Counter(b_tokens)
    inter = sum((a_counts & b_counts).values())
    if inter==0:
        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    prec = inter / sum(a_counts.values())
    rec = inter / sum(b_counts.values())
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec) if (prec+rec)>0 else 0.0
    return f1, prec, rec

best = []
for entry in data:
    cand = entry['ayah_text']
    cand_norm = normalize_ar(cand)
    tokens_cand = cand_norm.split()
    f1, prec, rec = token_f12(tokens_full, tokens_cand)
    lcs_len = lcs_length(full_norm, cand_norm)
    lcs_ratio_input = lcs_len / len(full_norm) if len(full_norm)>0 else 0
    lcs_ratio_cand = lcs_len / len(cand_norm) if len(cand_norm)>0 else 0
    composite = max(f1, lcs_ratio_input, lcs_ratio_cand)
    best.append((composite, f1, lcs_ratio_input, lcs_ratio_cand, entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], cand))
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:20]

best_by_f1 = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
best_by_f1[:20]

# find entry for 33:35 in our best_by_f1 list
[x for x in best_by_f1 if x[4]==33 and x[5]==35]

# load hadith books
p2 = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
with open(p2, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    hadiths = json.load(f)
len(hadiths), hadiths[:2]

# Compute token F1 for Quran and Hadith DBs and return top matches
def top_matches_quran(n=10):
    results = []
    for entry in data:
        cand = entry['ayah_text']
        cand_norm = normalize_ar(cand)
        tokens_cand = cand_norm.split()
        f1, prec, rec = token_f12(tokens_full, tokens_cand)
        results.append((f1, prec, rec, entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['ayah_text']))
    results_sorted = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    return results_sorted[:n]

def top_matches_hadith(n=10):
    results = []
    for entry in hadiths:
        matn = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
        cand_norm = normalize_ar(matn)
        tokens_cand = cand_norm.split()
        f1, prec, rec = token_f12(tokens_full, tokens_cand)
        results.append((f1, prec, rec, entry.get('hadithID'), entry.get('BookID'), matn[:80]))
    results_sorted = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    return results_sorted[:n]

top_q = top_matches_quran(20)
top_h = top_matches_hadith(20)
top_q, top_h[:10]

# compute lcs_ratio_input for all quran entries and find best
best_lcs_input = []
for entry in data:
    cand_norm = normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    lcs_len = lcs_length(full_norm, cand_norm)
    lcs_ratio_input = lcs_len / len(full_norm) if len(full_norm)>0 else 0
    best_lcs_input.append((lcs_ratio_input, entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['ayah_text']))
best_lcs_input_sorted = sorted(best_lcs_input, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_lcs_input_sorted[:20]

# compute unique token F1 for candidate 33:35
cand_norm = normalize_ar(candidate)
tokens_cand = cand_norm.split()
set_full_unique = set(tokens_full)
set_cand_unique = set(tokens_cand)
inter_unique = set_full_unique & set_cand_unique
prec_u = len(inter_unique)/len(set_full_unique)
rec_u = len(inter_unique)/len(set_cand_unique)
f1_u = 2*prec_u*rec_u/(prec_u+rec_u) if (prec_u+rec_u)>0 else 0
len(set_full_unique), len(set_cand_unique), len(inter_unique), prec_u, rec_u, f1_u, set_full_unique, inter_unique
